observed_facts: Terminal rewards r_stable_landing_once paid exactly once per episode in all 10 eval episodes
One-time bonuses remain strictly one-time per episode
Dominant component r_landing_quality (ratio 0.317), all shaping components trigger every step
Episode length mean 264.4 steps, contact toggle mean 9.8, max 20
Hack flags: only unstable_contact_behavior = true; all others false
likely_false_positives: Unstable contact behaviour flag is not a reward hack because terminal bonuses are one-time and not triggered repeatedly by bouncing.

lesson_assessments.reason: The atomic rebalancing that sharply reduced shaping weights and relaxed terminal durations was applied in the previous iteration and produced the current schema. Further application of the same edits would be redundant and could degrade the current 1.0 success rate.

avoid_actions: Do not alter one-time terminal bonuses to become repeatable.
Avoid reducing shaping weights further without evidence of new exploitation; current balance already yields perfect success.
recommended_actions: Continue training on the current schema to consolidate the landing policy.
Monitor for any degradation in success rate or emergence of new reward hacking; if success stays high, no edit needed.

reward_editor.rationale: The current reward schema yields 100% landing success with no reward hacking. The only diagnostic flag (unstable_contact_behavior) is a false positive that does not represent exploitation. The reflection strategy and memory assessment agree that no edit is needed; further training will refine the policy and may reduce bouncing without any reward modification.
reward_editor.expected_effect: Further training should maintain the 100% success rate, potentially reduce contact toggling and episode length, and increase landing precision. Hacking risk remains null because terminal bonuses are strictly one-time.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: Minimal risk. Over-training could cause slight overfitting or conservatism, but the reward structure prevents reward inflation. No degradation in success is expected; monitoring is sufficient.

what_worked: Atomic rebalancing that jointly cut dense shaping weights, clipped r_approach_region, and relaxed terminal event durations shifted a hovering/exploitation policy to successful landings (lesson_iter_000_to_001).
avoid_next: Solo decrease_weight on a single shaping component.
Making terminal bonuses repeatable or relaxing one-time constraints.
Reducing shaping weights further when success rate is already 1.0 and no new exploitation is present.
recommend_next: When success rate is high and hacking is absent, continue training rather than edit.
Monitor semantic metrics; only edit if success drops or new hacking emerges.
applicability_notes: The past lesson was effective when shaping dominance caused zero landings; it is not applicable now because the current schema already achieved its intended goal.
The current schema is an evolved version of that lesson and does not need re-application.

diagnosis: Current schema achieves 100% landing success with no reward hacking; only benign unstable contact. Continue training to further refine policy.
